title: gh-127208: Reject null character in _imp.create_dynamic()
author: vstinner
body:
_imp.create_dynamic() now rejects embedded null characters in the path and in the module name.

comments.author: picnixz
comments.body: According to the issue, it fails on 3.12 so does it require a 3.12 bp as well? (I don't have the code in front of me)

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body:
> According to the issue, it fails on 3.12 so does it require a 3.12 bp as well? (I don't have the code in front of me)

`_PyUnicode_AsUTF8NoNUL()` was added to Python 3.13. Since the bug report comes from a fuzzing tool, I don't think that it's worth it to backport the fix up to Python 3.12. The bug is old and nobody complained before.

comments.author: miss-islington-app[bot]
comments.body:
Thanks @vstinner for the PR 🌮🎉.. I'm working now to backport this PR to: 3.13.
🐍🍒⛏🤖

comments.author: bedevere-app[bot]
comments.body: [GH-127418](https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/127418) is a backport of this pull request to the [3.13 branch](https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/3.13).

comments.author: bedevere-app[bot]
comments.body: [GH-127419](https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/127419) is a backport of this pull request to the [3.12 branch](https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/3.12).

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: Hum, I changed my mind and backported the _PyUnicode_AsUTF8NoNUL() function instead :-) I created https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/127419 backport for 3.12.
